final_prompt.prompt:
Create an editorial architectural photograph of a famous Zen temple's karesansui dry landscape garden in Kyoto, Japan.

Style/medium: architectural photography, photorealistic, wide-angle lens, inspired by traditional Japanese garden documentation
Composition: panoramic wide-angle view, low-angle shot emphasizing geometric patterns and spatial depth, foreground to background layering
Subject: meticulously raked white gravel forming concentric circles and ripple patterns symbolizing ocean waves, moss-covered rocks of varying shapes representing islands and mountains
Scene/backdrop: low wooden corridor with evenly spaced pillars casting orderly shadows, ancient earthen wall with moss on top, autumn afternoon setting
Lighting/mood: warm afternoon slanting sunlight, elongated shadows on rocks, gravel rendered in warm off-white tones, profoundly serene and meditative Zen atmosphere
Color palette: warm off-white gravel as base, muted greens of moss, earthy browns of wood and earthen wall, subdued and restrained tonal range
Materials/textures: fine white gravel with precise raked patterns, soft moss on weathered rocks, aged wood grain, rough earthen wall surface
Avoid: oversaturated colors, modern elements, people, harsh midday light, 8K stuffer aesthetic, artificial enhancement
